coreTruthSet.description: Ouachita are framed as an environmental variation within the broader system rather than a dramatic civilizational break.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: The Ouachita (Washita) ... an environmental variation of the system.

coreTruthSet.description: Settlement pattern is portrayed as terrain-shaped and irregular, requiring navigation across localized micro-communities.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: More irregular distribution ... navigate multiple micro-communities.

coreTruthSet.description: Trade role is framed as important regional connector behavior without central-system dominance.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Regional connector, not central hub ... important-but not dominant.

coreTruthSet.description: Diplomacy is described as measured and slightly reserved, with slower trust formation that yields stronger durability.
Ouachita geography is characterized as a river-valley plus upland mix, producing less uniform terrain and slower movement.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: River-valley + upland mix ... movement is more varied and slower.

coreTruthSet.description: Language is treated as closely related to the baseline but with subtle regional inflection discernible to careful listeners.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Very close linguistically ... slight regional inflection in speech patterns.

settingAnchors.name: Ouachita River-Upland Interface
settingAnchors.description: Mixed-terrain contact zone combining river corridors with upland forest/hill movement constraints.

continuityConstraints.title: Micro-Community Navigation
continuityConstraints.text: Effective engagement requires treating Ouachita spaces as multiple local clusters rather than a single negotiable unit.

continuityConstraints.title: Terrain-Dependent Mobility Logic
continuityConstraints.text: Movement strategy shifts from route-first assumptions to terrain-conditioned pacing, pathing, and contact timing.

openResearchGaps.title: What specific speech-pattern cues can safely signal Ouachita regional inflection without over-claiming divergence?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed for dialogue differentiation grounded in nuanced linguistic proximity.

openResearchGaps.title: Which micro-community clusters are most relevant to Ouachita settlement and decision flow in the target period?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to convert terrain-shaped dispersion into concrete interaction maps for scenes and chapters.

openResearchGaps.title: What goods and reciprocal obligations most strongly define Ouachita regional connector trade behavior?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed for precise exchange scenes and realistic leverage dynamics.
